task_completion.description: Create the required layers for the drawing
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Layer List widget (usually on the right side of the LibreCAD interface). You should see layers named 'BOUNDARIES', 'HATCH-TOPSOIL', 'HATCH-CLAY', 'HATCH-GRAVEL', and 'TEXT'.

task_completion.description: Draw a 4m wide by 2m deep trench outline
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main drawing canvas. There should be a rectangular outline. Based on the grid or coordinate displays, it should be 4 units wide (X-axis) and 2 units deep/high (Y-axis).

task_completion.description: Draw polylines or splines to define three soil layers
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look inside the trench outline. There should be at least two dividing lines (straight or curved) that split the trench into three distinct sections (representing Topsoil, Clay, and Gravel).

task_completion.description: Apply hatch patterns to the soil layers
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for hatch patterns filling the three soil sections. The top section should have the 'ANSI31' pattern (diagonal parallel lines). The other two sections should also have hatch patterns applied (note: exact patterns requested for Clay/Gravel do not exist in LibreCAD, so any hatch pattern applied to them is acceptable).

task_completion.description: Add text labels for each soil context
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text strings 'TOPSOIL', 'CLAY', and 'GRAVEL' placed within or adjacent to their respective soil layers in the drawing area.
Look at the top window title bar of LibreCAD; it should display 'section_drawing.dxf'. Alternatively, look for a save dialog or terminal confirming the file was saved to /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD graphical user interface to create the drawing
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking tools, using the command line within LibreCAD, or interacting with LibreCAD dialog boxes (like the Hatch or Text dialogs), rather than writing a Python script to generate the DXF file.
The sequence of screenshots should show the drawing being built progressively (e.g., outline first, then dividing lines, then hatches), rather than the completed drawing appearing instantly.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually write DXF code in a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of the agent opening a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) to paste or write raw DXF code.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/section_drawing.dxf. Required layers are BOUNDARIES, HATCH-TOPSOIL, HATCH-CLAY, HATCH-GRAVEL, and TEXT. The trench outline must be 4 units wide (x: 0 to 4) and 2 units deep (y: 0 to 2). Required text labels are TOPSOIL, CLAY, and GRAVEL. CRITICAL: The hatch pattern ANSI31 exists in LibreCAD and should be used for Topsoil. However, ANSI37, AR-SAND, and ANSI33 DO NOT exist in LibreCAD. The agent cannot apply these exact patterns for Clay and Gravel. Do not penalize the agent if they use alternative hatch patterns for the Clay and Gravel layers, or if they struggle to find the non-existent patterns, as long as they attempt to hatch the areas.
